definition: Risk appetite operates in 5 discrete regimes with mandatory sequential transitions. Complacent carry cannot skip directly to systemic deleveraging; intermediate regimes must be traversed in order.
evidence: Panel consensus on ordered regime transitions
Historical pattern analysis showing no regime-skip episodes
Sequenced transition rule validated across multiple market stress periods
open_questions: What are the precise boundary conditions between each of the 5 regimes?
Can policy intervention reverse regime transitions or only slow them?
What is the average dwell time in each regime?
